engineIntelligence.h2: Dodge Durango Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Dodge Durango in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Dodge Durango ERB engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
productionHistory: Pre-facelift WK2 and facelift WD chassis - 2011-2024
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - tensioner wear causes rattling noise from 60k-100k miles.
Water pump failure - internal leak leads to coolant loss and overheating risk.
Cam phaser rattle - VVT actuator wear on cold start, common after 80k miles.

cards.altText: Dodge Durango EZH engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Lifter tick - collapsed hydraulic lifters cause ticking noise, often 70k+ miles.
Valve seat recession - intake valve seat dropout causes compression loss and misfire.
MDS solenoid failure - cylinder deactivation faults trigger rough idle and warning lights.

cards.altText: Dodge Durango EXF engine - diesel 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR cooler crack - thermal fatigue causes coolant leak into intake, risk of combustion.
Injector carbon buildup - diesel injector coking leads to misfire and rough running.
DPF regeneration issues - blocked filter from short-journey driving causes power loss.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £4,800-£6,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EXF units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Dodge Durango Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Dodge Durango across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: The most common UK Durango engine, introduced 2011 with aluminium block and VVT for efficiency.
compatibleVariants: SXT, GT, Citadel, AWD, RWD, WK2, WD
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch with tensioner wear - documented from 60k-100k miles on UK forums
Water pump internal seal failure - coolant loss without external leak, common after 80k miles
Cam phaser/VVT actuator rattle on cold start - wear item requiring replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Updated Pentastar code for facelift WD chassis with refined calibration and emissions compliance.
compatibleVariants: SXT WD, GT WD, Citadel WD, AWD WD
commonFailures: Timing chain system wear - same root cause as ERB, preventative replacement recommended
Water pump failure - internal leak pattern consistent across Pentastar family
Oil consumption on higher mileage units - monitor levels between services
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESG Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production Pentastar variant with updated PCM mapping for improved drivability.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented on UK owner forums for pre-2020 units
Water pump internal leak - same failure pattern as other Pentastar codes
VVT solenoid clogging - oil quality dependent, causes rough idle if neglected
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESH Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The flagship HEMI for UK Durango, featuring MDS cylinder deactivation for fuel economy.
compatibleVariants: R/T, Citadel, GT Plus, AWD, RWD, WK2, WD
commonFailures: Hydraulic lifter collapse - "lifter tick" common from 70k miles, requires full valvetrain inspection
Valve seat recession - intake seat dropout causes compression loss, documented on high-mileage units
MDS solenoid failure - cylinder deactivation faults trigger warning lights and rough running
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZH Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Updated HEMI code with revised MDS calibration for improved reliability and emissions.
compatibleVariants: R/T WD, Citadel WD, GT Plus WD, AWD WD
commonFailures: Lifter tick - same root cause as EZH, preventative inspection recommended at 60k miles
MDS solenoid wear - updated parts available, but failure pattern remains similar
Exhaust manifold bolt breakage - heat cycling causes leaks, common on pre-2020 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZH-T Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Early HEMI code for pre-facelift WK2 Durango, foundational MDS implementation.
compatibleVariants: R/T WK2, Citadel WK2, AWD WK2
commonFailures: Lifter collapse - well-documented on UK forums for 2011-2015 units
Valve seat recession - higher incidence on early production engines
Timing chain guide wear - plastic guides can degrade, causing noise and potential failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGG Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 6.4L Petrol (392)
entries.history: The 392 HEMI brought SRT-level performance to the Durango, with forged internals and high-flow heads.
compatibleVariants: SRT 392, SRT 392 Plus, SRT 392 Premium, AWD only
commonFailures: Valve spring fatigue - high-RPM use can cause spring failure, documented on performance forums
Piston ring land wear - aggressive driving accelerates wear, monitor compression on high-mileage units
Oil consumption under load - normal for performance engine but requires vigilant monitoring
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESG 392 Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 6.2L Supercharged Petrol (Hellcat)
entries.history: The supercharged Hellcat V8 made the Durango the most powerful SUV in its class, with specialised calibration.
compatibleVariants: SRT Hellcat, SRT Hellcat Hammerhead, SRT Hellcat Silver Bullet, SRT Hellcat Brass Monkey
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler wear - high torque loads can cause driveline lash, requires inspection
Heat exchanger efficiency loss - cooling system critical for sustained performance, monitor temperatures
Fuel pump demand - high-pressure pump wear under aggressive use, replacement recommended at 50k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for EJH Hellcat Dodge Durango engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The VM Motori-sourced EcoDiesel offered strong torque and fuel economy for UK Durango buyers, with complex emissions systems.
compatibleVariants: EcoDiesel AWD, EcoDiesel GT, EcoDiesel Citadel, WK2 chassis only
commonFailures: EGR cooler crack - thermal fatigue causes coolant leak into intake, NHTSA recall W79 applies
Injector carbon buildup - diesel injector coking leads to misfire, requires professional cleaning or replacement
DPF regeneration issues - blocked filter from short-journey driving causes power loss and warning lights
entries.cta: Get quotes for EXF EcoDiesel Dodge Durango engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Dodge Durango Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Dodge Durango's most common replacement engines in the UK are the 3.6L Pentastar V6 and 5.7L HEMI V8, with timing chain, lifter and valve seat issues driving enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: Pentastar Timing Chain Stretch - Why 3.6L Durango Engines Rattle Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Durango SXT, GT, Citadel (2011-2024, ERB/ESG/ESH Pentastar 3.6L)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling noise on cold start and potential jump if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Tensioner Replacement - Replace tensioner and guides before chain jump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove front cover, replace tensioner, guides and chain if stretched
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain jump; adds 40-60k miles if successful

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Pentastar Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioners
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with rebuilt unit featuring upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines with jump damage or over 90k miles; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: If your Durango shows early chain rattle under 70k miles and compression is good, a preventative tensioner job from a specialist offers better value. If the chain has jumped or mileage exceeds 90k, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Pentastar engine prices for your Dodge Durango

problems.h4: HEMI Lifter Tick - Why 5.7L Durango Engines Develop Ticking Noise
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Durango R/T, Citadel, GT Plus (2011-2024, EZH/EZH-T/EGG HEMI 5.7L)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lifter collapse due to oil aeration or wear, causing metallic ticking and potential camshaft damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Lifter & Cam Inspection - Replace collapsed lifters, inspect cam lobes
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve covers, replace affected lifters, inspect cam for scoring, MDS solenoid check
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if cam lobes are undamaged; risk of repeat if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HEMI Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded lifters and MDS components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with rebuilt HEMI featuring upgraded lifters, MDS solenoids, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines with cam damage, high mileage, or repeat lifter failures

problems.recommendation: On a Durango R/T worth £15k-£25k, if the cam is scored or mileage exceeds 90k, a rebuilt HEMI with upgraded lifters offers better long-term value than repeated lifter repairs. For early-stage tick under 75k miles with good oil history, specialist lifter replacement may suffice.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt HEMI engine for your Dodge Durango

problems.h4: HEMI Valve Seat Recession - Why Compression Drops on High-Mileage 5.7L Units
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Durango R/T, Citadel (2011-2017, EGG/EZH early HEMI 5.7L)
problems.rootCause: Intake valve seat dropout due to material fatigue, causing compression loss, misfire and potential piston damage.

repairOptions.tier: Major - Cylinder Head Repair - Remove heads, replace valve seats, valve job
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove both cylinder heads, machine seats, replace valves, reassemble with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block and pistons are undamaged; labour-intensive with repeat risk on early codes

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HEMI Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded valve seats
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with rebuilt HEMI featuring upgraded valve seats, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for engines with piston scoring or over 100k miles; eliminates repeat seat failure risk

problems.recommendation: Valve seat recession is a fundamental design limitation on early HEMI codes. If your Durango shows compression loss or misfire on one cylinder, a rebuilt engine with upgraded seats is the only reliable long-term solution - head repair alone rarely prevents recurrence.
problems.cta: Compare HEMI valve seat upgrade engine prices for your Dodge Durango

problems.h4: EcoDiesel EGR Cooler Failure - Why 3.0L Durango Diesel Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Durango EcoDiesel AWD, GT, Citadel (2014-2020, EXF EcoDiesel 3.0L)
problems.rootCause: Thermal fatigue cracks the EGR cooler, allowing coolant vapour into the intake where it can combust, risking catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cooler Replacement - Replace cooler with updated part, clean intake
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, replace EGR cooler with revised part, clean carbon from intake runners
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before combustion event; NHTSA recall W79 may cover some units

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EcoDiesel Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded EGR system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with rebuilt EcoDiesel featuring upgraded EGR components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if combustion damage occurred or mileage exceeds 100k; eliminates repeat cooler failure risk

problems.recommendation: If your EcoDiesel Durango shows coolant loss without external leak or white smoke, stop driving immediately and have the EGR cooler inspected. If combustion damage is confirmed, only a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers reliable long-term protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt EcoDiesel engine for your Dodge Durango

problems.h4: Pentastar Water Pump Failure - Why 3.6L Durango Engines Lose Coolant Internally
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Durango SXT, GT, Citadel (2011-2024, ERB/ESG/ESH Pentastar 3.6L)
problems.rootCause: Internal seal failure in the water pump allows coolant to leak into the oil system or externally without visible puddle.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Water Pump Replacement - Replace pump with updated seal design, flush system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove accessory drive, replace water pump with revised part, flush cooling system, refill with correct coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before oil contamination or overheating; adds 50-70k miles with proper maintenance

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Pentastar Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded water pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with rebuilt Pentastar featuring upgraded water pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil contamination occurred, engine overheated, or mileage exceeds 100k

problems.recommendation: If your Pentastar Durango shows coolant loss without external leak but no overheating or oil contamination, a specialist water pump replacement with the updated seal design is cost-effective. If the engine overheated or shows milky oil, a rebuilt engine is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Pentastar water pump upgrade prices for your Dodge Durango

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Dodge Durango
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Durango suffers from Pentastar timing chain rattle, HEMI lifter tick, or EcoDiesel EGR failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand Dodge's proprietary MDS and PCM systems. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Dodge Durango Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Dodge Durango and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Dodge Durango Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Dodge Durango in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Dodge Durango 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 powered most UK Durango variants from 2011, offering a balance of power and efficiency with VVT technology; it remains the most commonly replaced engine due to timing chain and water pump failure patterns.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle; internal water pump seal failure leading to coolant loss
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge Durango 3.6L petrol engines

items.title: Dodge Durango 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.7L HEMI V8 brought V8 power to UK Durango R/T and Citadel trims, featuring MDS cylinder deactivation for fuel economy but introducing lifter and valve seat failure risks on higher-mileage units.
commonFailurePoints: Hydraulic lifter collapse causing metallic tick; intake valve seat recession leading to compression loss
items.cta: Compare Dodge Durango 5.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge Durango 6.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.4L 392 HEMI delivered SRT-level performance to the Durango from 2018, with forged internals and high-flow heads, but requires specialist knowledge for replacement due to performance-specific calibration.
commonFailurePoints: Valve spring fatigue under high-RPM use; piston ring land wear with aggressive driving
items.cta: Compare Dodge Durango 6.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge Durango 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The supercharged 6.2L Hellcat V8 made the Durango the most powerful SUV in its class from 2021, with specialised PCM calibration and cooling requirements that demand expert handling during replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Supercharger coupler wear under high torque loads; heat exchanger efficiency loss affecting sustained performance
items.cta: Compare Dodge Durango 6.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Dodge Durango 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L EcoDiesel V6 offered strong torque and fuel economy for UK Durango buyers from 2014-2020, but complex emissions systems including the EGR cooler introduced specific failure patterns requiring specialist attention.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler thermal fatigue causing coolant leak into intake; injector carbon buildup leading to misfire
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge Durango 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Dodge Durango Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L EcoDiesel V6 was offered in limited UK Durango variants from 2014-2020, delivering strong torque and fuel economy but requiring specialist knowledge for EGR and DPF system maintenance.
items.descriptor: The 3.0L EcoDiesel V6 was offered in limited UK Durango variants from 2014-2020, delivering strong torque and fuel economy but requiring specialist knowledge for EGR and DPF system maintenance.

foundIn: WK2 chassis EcoDiesel variants (2014-2020) - EXF code only
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for towing and motorway cruising
EGR cooler thermal fatigue causing coolant leak into intake - NHTSA recall W79 applies
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey UK driving patterns
Limited UK availability means fewer replacement options versus petrol engines

items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominate the UK Durango range, with the 3.6L Pentastar V6 and 5.7L HEMI V8 covering most variants, plus high-performance 6.4L and 6.2L options for SRT models.

engineYears.h2: Dodge Durango Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Dodge Durango's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The third-generation Durango launched in the UK with the new Pentastar 3.6L V6 and optional 5.7L HEMI V8, establishing the engine line-up that would define the model for a decade.
keyChanges: Introduction of WK2 chassis with unibody construction replacing body-on-frame
Pentastar 3.6L V6 (ERB) becomes standard engine across SXT, GT, Citadel trims
5.7L HEMI V8 (EGG/EZH) optional on R/T and Citadel with MDS cylinder deactivation
8-speed automatic transmission replaces previous 5-speed across all variants
mainEngines: EZH - 5.7L petrol HEMI with updated MDS calibration (late 2014+)
knownFor: Pentastar timing chain tensioner wear emerges as common high-mileage issue
Early HEMI codes (EGG) show higher incidence of lifter tick versus updated EZH
Strong towing capability with HEMI, but fuel economy remains a consideration for UK buyers
Grey import verification critical - many UK units are US-spec with different emissions calibration
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2015 Dodge Durango replacement engines
years.ticker: Dodge Durango 2014 Pentastar timing chain replacement • Dodge Durango 2013 HEMI lifter tick fix • Dodge Durango 2015 engine code compatibility check
enquiries: Dodge Durango 2014 Pentastar timing chain replacement
Dodge Durango 2013 HEMI lifter tick fix
Dodge Durango 2015 engine code compatibility check

years.preview: WK2 Matures: Refined Calibration & EcoDiesel Arrival
years.description: Mid-cycle updates brought refined PCM calibration for Pentastar and HEMI engines, plus the limited UK introduction of the 3.0L EcoDiesel V6 for improved fuel economy.
keyChanges: Updated Pentastar codes (ESH) with revised PCM mapping for improved drivability
HEMI MDS calibration refined (EZH-T) to reduce lifter failure incidence
EcoDiesel 3.0L V6 (EXF) introduced for UK market on AWD variants only
Infotainment and safety tech updates, but powertrain architecture unchanged
mainEngines: EZH/EZH-T - 5.7L petrol HEMI (R/T / Citadel / GT Plus)
EXF - 3.0L diesel EcoDiesel (EcoDiesel AWD / GT / Citadel)
knownFor: Pentastar water pump internal seal failure pattern becomes well-documented on UK forums
HEMI valve seat recession emerges on high-mileage early codes (EGG/EZH pre-2017)
EcoDiesel EGR cooler thermal fatigue leads to NHTSA recall W79 - critical for UK owners to verify compliance
PCM calibration critical post-replacement for MDS/VVT systems to avoid drivability issues
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2020 Dodge Durango replacement engines
years.ticker: Dodge Durango 2018 Pentastar water pump replacement • Dodge Durango 2017 HEMI valve seat fix • Dodge Durango 2019 EcoDiesel EGR cooler recall
enquiries: Dodge Durango 2018 Pentastar water pump replacement
Dodge Durango 2017 HEMI valve seat fix
Dodge Durango 2019 EcoDiesel EGR cooler recall

years.preview: WD Facelift: Performance Expansion & Updated Codes
years.description: The facelifted WD chassis brought updated engine codes across the range, plus the arrival of the high-performance 6.4L 392 and 6.2L Hellcat HEMI for UK SRT variants.
keyChanges: Updated Pentastar code ESG with refined emissions calibration for WD chassis
HEMI EZH-T becomes standard code for 5.7L applications with improved MDS reliability
SRT 392 (6.4L ESG) and SRT Hellcat (6.2L EJH) introduced for UK market
Enhanced PCM software requiring specialist calibration tools for post-replacement setup
mainEngines: ESG - 3.6L petrol Pentastar (SXT / GT / Citadel WD)
EZH-T - 5.7L petrol HEMI (R/T / Citadel / GT Plus WD)
EJH - 6.2L supercharged petrol HEMI (SRT Hellcat WD)
knownFor: Updated Pentastar ESG shows improved tensioner durability versus early ERB codes
HEMI EZH-T reduces but does not eliminate lifter tick risk versus early EGG/EZH
SRT 392 and Hellcat require specialist knowledge for PCM calibration and cooling system maintenance
Grey import verification remains critical - many UK WD chassis units are US-spec with different emissions calibration
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-2024 Dodge Durango replacement engines
years.ticker: Dodge Durango 2022 Pentastar ESG replacement • Dodge Durango 2023 HEMI EZH-T calibration • Dodge Durango 2024 SRT Hellcat engine availability
enquiries: Dodge Durango 2024 SRT Hellcat engine availability

years.description: First-generation Durango models were not officially imported to the UK; any pre-2011 units are grey imports requiring specialist verification for engine compatibility and emissions compliance.
keyChanges: WK1 chassis used body-on-frame construction with different engine mounting points versus WK2/WD
PowerTech 4.7L V8 and 5.9L V8 were common in US market but rare in UK
No official UK emissions certification for pre-2011 Durango variants
mainEngines: 5.9L Magnum V8 (very rare UK grey imports)
knownFor: Pre-2011 Durango engines require specialist verification for UK MoT emissions compliance
Parts availability limited versus WK2/WD chassis engines
PCM calibration differences between US-spec and UK requirements can cause drivability issues post-replacement
years.cta: Enquire about pre-2011 Dodge Durango grey import engine compatibility

faq.h2: Dodge Durango Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Dodge Durango engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine costs for the Dodge Durango range from £3,500 for the common 3.6L Pentastar to £5,800 for the 5.7L HEMI, with supply-only pricing; supply & fit adds £800-£1,800 depending on garage location.
keyPoints: 3.6L Pentastar rebuilt: £3,500-£5,500 supply only
5.7L HEMI rebuilt: £4,200-£5,800 supply only
EcoDiesel 3.0L rebuilt: £4,800-£6,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £800-£1,800 to engine price
items.cta: Compare Dodge Durango engine replacement prices UK

items.question: What engine codes fit my Dodge Durango?
items.answer: Your Dodge Durango's engine code is identified by the 8th digit of the VIN: ERB/ESG/ESH for 3.6L Pentastar, EZH/EZH-T/EGG for 5.7L HEMI, EXF for 3.0L EcoDiesel, ESG (392) for 6.4L, and EJH for 6.2L Hellcat.
keyPoints: Check VIN 8th digit for exact engine code match
Pentastar codes: ERB, ESG, ESH, ESA, ESB, ESD, ESJ
HEMI codes: EZH, EZH-T, EGG, ESH HEMI, 9EZH
Use EngineMarket's VIN decoder to verify compatibility instantly
items.cta: Verify your Dodge Durango engine code compatibility now

items.question: How long does a Dodge Durango engine replacement take?
items.answer: A supply-only Dodge Durango engine typically ships within 3-5 working days from UK stock; supply & fit installations take 2-4 days at specialist garages, depending on parts availability and PCM calibration requirements.
keyPoints: Supply-only engines: 3-5 working days from UK stock
Supply & fit installations: 2-4 days at specialist garages
PCM/MDS calibration adds 0.5-1 day post-install
Complex variants like SRT Hellcat may require longer lead times
items.cta: Check Dodge Durango engine availability and turnaround times

items.question: Do you offer warranty on Dodge Durango replacement engines?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt or reconditioned Dodge Durango engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Coverage includes MDS solenoids, VVT actuators, and upgraded failure-point components
Warranty valid at any UK garage; keep service records for claims
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge Durango engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I use a used engine in my Dodge Durango?
items.answer: Yes, used Dodge Durango engines are available from £2,200 supply only, but require careful verification of mileage, compression test results, and service history to avoid repeat failure.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £2,200-£4,200 supply only versus £3,500+ for rebuilt
Always request compression test results and service records before purchase
Used engines typically carry 3-6 month warranty versus 12 months for rebuilt
Best suited for low-budget repairs where engine history is fully documented
items.cta: Compare used versus rebuilt Dodge Durango engine options

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Dodge Durango MoT?
items.answer: A compliant replacement engine will not affect your Dodge Durango's MoT, but emissions-legal engines with correct catalytic converters and O2 sensors are essential for UK MoT pass, especially on grey imports.
keyPoints: Replacement engines must include MoT-compliant catalytic converters and O2 sensors
Grey import Durangos require emissions verification for UK MoT compliance
EcoDiesel variants must have functional DPF and EGR systems to pass emissions test
EngineMarket suppliers ensure all engines meet UK emissions standards
items.cta: Enquire about MoT-compliant Dodge Durango replacement engines

items.question: Do I need ECU reprogramming after Durango engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, Dodge Durango engines with MDS or VVT systems require PCM calibration after replacement to ensure proper cylinder deactivation, variable timing, and drivability; specialist tools are needed for this process.
keyPoints: MDS solenoids require calibration to avoid rough idle or warning lights
VVT actuators need PCM reset for correct timing advance/retard
SRT variants with performance calibration require specialised software
EngineMarket network garages include PCM calibration in supply & fit packages
items.cta: Find Dodge Durango specialists offering PCM calibration service

items.question: How do I know if my Durango needs a rebuilt or used engine?
items.answer: Choose a rebuilt Dodge Durango engine if your failure involves timing chain jump, lifter collapse, or valve seat recession; a used engine may suffice for low-mileage units with minor wear and documented service history.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines recommended for catastrophic failures or engines over 90k miles
Used engines suitable for low-mileage units with minor wear and full history
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points
Consider vehicle value: rebuilt engines offer better long-term value for higher-spec variants
items.cta: Get expert advice on rebuilt vs used Dodge Durango engines

items.question: Can you supply and fit a Dodge Durango engine near me?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network includes over 100 vetted UK specialists offering supply & fit Dodge Durango engine replacement with nationwide coverage, PCM calibration capability, and warranty on parts and labour.
keyPoints: Over 100 vetted UK garages in EngineMarket network
Nationwide coverage including London, Manchester, Birmingham, Glasgow
Supply & fit packages include engine, labour, fluids, and PCM calibration
All work backed by minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Find Dodge Durango engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What problems do Dodge Durango engines commonly have?
items.answer: The most common Dodge Durango engine problems in the UK are Pentastar timing chain stretch (60k-100k miles), HEMI lifter tick (70k-110k miles), and EcoDiesel EGR cooler failure (80k-120k miles).
keyPoints: Pentastar 3.6L: Timing chain tensioner wear, water pump internal leak
HEMI 5.7L: Hydraulic lifter collapse, valve seat recession, MDS solenoid failure
EcoDiesel 3.0L: EGR cooler thermal fatigue, injector carbon buildup, DPF issues
Early intervention on warning signs can prevent catastrophic engine damage
items.cta: Compare Dodge Durango engine problem repair costs UK
